title: Add Mixed Numbers with Decimal Fractions
description: Add mixed numbers by combining whole numbers and fractional parts separately.
condition: Two mixed numbers with tenths and/or hundredths to be added.
response: Add whole numbers together and add fractional parts (using common denominators if needed), then combine.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Includes regrouping when fractional sum exceeds 1.

title: Align Decimal Places to Compare
description: Compare decimals by aligning place values (including adding trailing zeros if needed) and comparing digits from left to right.
condition: Two decimal numbers with the same whole number part or different numbers of decimal places are given for comparison.
response: Rewrite decimals with equal place values if needed and compare digits from greatest place value to least to determine >, <, or =.
label: Principle / Model
example: Compare $0.3$ and $0.24$. Rewrite $0.3$ as $0.30$. Since $30$ hundredths $>$ $24$ hundredths, $0.30 > 0.24$.
notes: Includes understanding that trailing zeros do not change value.

title: Compare Decimals Using Place Value
description: Compare two decimal numbers by examining digits from left to right according to place value.
condition: Two decimal numbers are given with a comparison prompt (>, <, =).
response: Determine which decimal is greater, less, or equal using place value.
label: Principle
notes: Students may rewrite decimals with equal digits (e.g., 0.4 as 0.40).

title: Convert Decimal to Fraction (Place Value)
description: Write a terminating decimal as a fraction using its place value (tenths, hundredths).
condition: A terminating decimal with one or two decimal places is given.
response: Write the decimal as a fraction with denominator 10 or 100 based on place value.
label: Principle
notes: May be expressed as mixed numbers. [merged: condition varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Convert Decimal to Mixed Fraction
description: Convert a decimal with whole number into a mixed number using tenths or hundredths.
condition: Decimal number with whole part and decimal part (e.g., 2.65).
response: Write as whole number plus fraction based on place value (e.g., hundredths).
label: Principle / Model
notes: Used to select correct expression in Q7 Part A.

title: Convert Mixed Number with Tenths to Decimal
description: Convert a mixed number with denominator 10 into decimal form by combining whole number and tenths.
condition: A mixed number of the form w a/10
response: Write the decimal w.a
label: Principle / Model
notes: Builds on place value and fraction-to-decimal conversion. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies]

title: Convert Tenths/Hundredths Fraction to Decimal
description: Convert a fraction with denominator 10 or 100 into decimal form using place value.
condition: A fraction with denominator 10 or 100 is given.
response: Write the numerator in the tenths or hundredths place to form the decimal.
label: Principle
notes: May require adding a leading zero (e.g., $\frac{5}{100} = 0.05$).

title: Decimal–Fraction Equivalence (Tenths/Hundredths)
description: Recognize that decimals represent fractions with denominators 10 or 100.
condition: A decimal with one or two decimal places (e.g., 0.4, 0.39).
response: Express as an equivalent fraction with denominator 10 or 100.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Place value determines denominator (10 or 100).

title: Ensure Decimals Refer to the Same Whole
description: When comparing decimals using visual models, confirm that both representations refer to the same-sized whole.
condition: Two visual decimal models (e.g., beakers, grids) are shown for comparison.
response: Verify that each model represents one whole of the same size before comparing shaded amounts.
label: Concept
example: To compare $0.5$ and $0.14$, ensure both beakers are marked from $0$ to $1$ with the same scale before judging which is greater.
notes: Critical for valid visual comparison.

title: Identify Fraction from Hundred Grid Model
description: Determine the fraction represented by shaded squares in a 10×10 grid.
condition: A 10×10 area model with some squares shaded is shown.
response: Count shaded squares and write as a fraction out of 100.
label: Principle
example: If 19 squares are shaded, the fraction is $\frac{19}{100}$.
notes: Each large square represents 1 whole.

title: Identify Fraction from Tenths Area Model
description: Determine the fraction represented by shaded parts when a whole is partitioned into 10 equal parts.
condition: An area model divided into 10 equal parts with some parts shaded
response: Count shaded parts and write shaded_count/10
label: Principle / Model
example: If 8 of 10 parts are shaded, the fraction is \frac{8}{10}.
notes: Requires equal partition recognition.

title: Interpret 1 Meter as One Whole
description: Interpret a length model labeled 1 meter as representing one whole partitioned into equal hundredths.
condition: A bar labeled 1 meter partitioned into 100 equal parts.
response: Treat each small part as $\frac{1}{100}$ meter and total shaded parts accordingly.
label: Principle / Model
example: 13 shaded parts out of 100 → $\frac{13}{100}$ m = 0.13 m.
notes: Connects measurement model to place value.

title: Interpret Hundredths Grid Model
description: Interpret a 10×10 grid as representing 100 hundredths and determine the decimal from shaded squares.
condition: A 10×10 grid with some squares shaded is shown.
response: Count shaded squares and express the amount as a decimal to the hundredths place.
label: Principle / Model
example: If $38$ squares are shaded, the decimal is $0.38$ since $38$ hundredths equals $0.38$.
notes: Relies on place value understanding of hundredths.

title: Match Equivalent Fraction and Decimal
description: Recognize and pair equivalent fraction and decimal representations.
condition: Given a fraction and a set of decimal choices (or vice versa).
response: Select the decimal (or fraction) that represents the same value.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Requires understanding of place value equivalence.

title: Match Equivalent Fraction Representations
description: Recognize when two fractions represent the same quantity despite different forms.
condition: Two fractions with different numerators and/or denominators presented for comparison.
response: Identify or select the fraction that is equivalent in value.
label: Concept
notes: Response is selection of a matching value from choices.

title: Order Decimals from Least to Greatest
description: Arrange a set of decimals in ascending order by comparing place values.
condition: Three or more decimal numbers are given with the instruction to order from least to greatest.
response: Compare decimals pairwise using place value and list them from smallest to largest.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Often requires aligning decimal places (e.g., $0.4 = 0.40$).

title: Plot Decimal on Number Line
description: Locate and label a decimal number correctly on a number line between benchmarks.
condition: A number line with benchmarks (e.g., 0 and 1) and a decimal number is given.
response: Place a point at the position corresponding to the decimal’s value.
label: Production / Skill
example: Place $0.25$ one quarter of the way from 0 to 1 and $0.4$ at four tenths.
notes: Requires proportional spatial reasoning. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies]

title: Plot Tenths Fraction on Number Line
description: Locate and label a fraction with denominator 10 on a number line from 0 to 1 partitioned into tenths.
condition: A number line from 0 to 1 divided into 10 equal intervals and a fraction a/10
response: Place a point at the ath tick mark after 0 and label it a/10
label: Production / Skill
example: For \frac{4}{10}, place the point at the 4th tick after 0.
notes: Spatial placement skill tied to counting intervals.

title: Read Measurement Scale in Tenths
description: Interpret a measurement scale marked in tenths to determine the quantity shown.
condition: A vertical or horizontal scale from 0 to 1 divided into 10 equal parts with a marked level
response: Count tenths to the marked level and state the value as a/10 or decimal 0.a
label: Production / Skill
example: If the liquid reaches 6 of 10 marks, the amount is \frac{6}{10} = 0.6.
notes: Requires interpreting visual scale markings.

title: Rewrite Decimal with Trailing Zero
description: Add trailing zeros to a decimal without changing its value to compare place values.
condition: Decimal numbers with different numbers of decimal digits to compare.
response: Append zero(s) to shorter decimal to equalize place values.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Supports comparison and addition.

title: Select Expression Matching Word Problem
description: Choose the numerical expression that correctly represents quantities described in a word problem.
condition: A word problem describes quantities and asks for an expression.
response: Identify the expression that correctly represents the situation.
label: Concept
notes: Response is constant for the specific problem.

title: Tenths to Hundredths Equivalence
description: Rename a fraction with denominator 10 as an equivalent fraction with denominator 100.
condition: A fraction of the form $\frac{a}{10}$ to be expressed in hundredths.
response: Multiply numerator and denominator by 10 to get $\frac{10a}{100}$.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Based on equivalent fractions using ×10/×10. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies]

title: Use Comparison Symbols for Decimals
description: Select and write the correct comparison symbol (> , < , =) after comparing two decimals.
condition: Two decimal numbers have been compared and their relative size is known.
response: Write the correct symbol (> , < , =) between the numbers.
label: Rule / Plan
notes: Symbol selection follows from prior comparison.

title: Write Decimal in Unit Form
description: Express a decimal as a combination of place value units (e.g., tenths, hundredths).
condition: A decimal to the tenths or hundredths place is given.
response: State the value in unit form using place value language.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Supports reasoning about decimal magnitude.

title: Write Fraction and Decimal Forms
description: Express a quantity in both fraction (tenths) and decimal form.
condition: A quantity given in either tenths fraction or decimal form with denominator 10 context
response: Provide both equivalent forms (a/10 and 0.a)
label: Principle / Model
notes: Combines conversion in both directions.
